prerequisites: A Snowflake account, and the account identifier used to build the connection host (for example orgname-account_name.snowflakecomputing.com or the legacy account_locator.region.cloud.snowflakecomputing.com form).
A Snowflake user to authenticate as, plus credentials for that user (a password, an RSA key pair, or a programmatic access token).
A role (set the required 'role' field) that has been granted to the user and holds the privileges needed to read the target data.
A running virtual warehouse (the required 'warehouse' field) that the role can use to execute queries, since compute is required to run any query.
The target database (the required 'database' field), and optionally a default schema (the 'schema' field) where the tables and views to read live.

authMethods.name: Username and password
authMethods.summary: Authenticate as a Snowflake user with that user's password. Maps to the secret field credentials.password. The password is set by an administrator with CREATE USER ... PASSWORD or ALTER USER.

authMethods.name: Key-pair authentication
authMethods.summary: Authenticate with an RSA private key instead of a password. Maps to credentials.private_key, and credentials.private_key_password when the private key is encrypted with a passphrase. Generate an RSA key pair, assign the public key to the user via ALTER USER ... SET RSA_PUBLIC_KEY, and supply the private key here. The passphrase only protects the local private key and is never sent to Snowflake.

authMethods.name: Programmatic access token (PAT)
authMethods.summary: Authenticate with a programmatic access token used in place of a password. Maps to credentials.programmatic_access_token. Generate the token in Snowsight or via ALTER USER ... ADD PROGRAMMATIC ACCESS TOKEN; the token secret is shown only once at creation. A network policy is required for service users (and to use a person user's token), and tokens can be scoped to a specific role.

setupSteps.title: Identify your account host
setupSteps.body: Determine your Snowflake account identifier and form the connection host. The preferred form is orgname-account_name.snowflakecomputing.com; the legacy form is account_locator.region.cloud.snowflakecomputing.com. Enter this in the required 'host' field. All access to Snowflake is through your account identifier.

setupSteps.title: Create or choose a user and role
setupSteps.body: Use (or have an administrator create) a Snowflake user to connect as, and a role granted to that user. Roles are Snowflake's access-control mechanism: grant the role the privileges needed to read your target objects. Set the role in the required 'role' field. Every user automatically has the PUBLIC role, but a dedicated role with explicit grants is recommended.

setupSteps.title: Provision a warehouse, database, and schema
setupSteps.body: Specify a running virtual warehouse in the required 'warehouse' field; compute is required to execute queries. Set the database to read in the required 'database' field. Optionally set a default 'schema' to scope which tables and views are read. Databases and schemas organize tables and views within Snowflake.

setupSteps.title: Configure authentication credentials
setupSteps.body: Provide one set of credentials under 'credentials': a password (credentials.password), an RSA private key with optional passphrase (credentials.private_key and credentials.private_key_password), or a programmatic access token (credentials.programmatic_access_token). For key-pair auth, first register the public key on the user with ALTER USER ... SET RSA_PUBLIC_KEY. For a PAT, generate it in Snowsight or via ALTER USER ... ADD PROGRAMMATIC ACCESS TOKEN and copy the secret immediately, since it is shown only once.

setupSteps.title: Tune optional connection settings
setupSteps.body: Optionally set 'jdbc_url_params' to pass extra JDBC connection parameters, 'check_privileges' to control whether table privileges are validated, 'cursor' for incremental reads, and performance options such as 'concurrency' and 'checkpoint_target_interval_seconds'. These are optional and not required for a basic connection.

sources.title: Managing users (CREATE USER, roles, default role/warehouse)
